statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.name: assistTurnoverRatio
statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

article.description: Xavier Cooks scored 19 points and grabbed eight rebounds and Joshua Davenport added 12 and 10 to help Winthrop beat Jacksonville State 80-69 Friday night.
article.headline: Cooks scores 19, Winthrop beats Jacksonville St. 80-69
article.linkText: Cooks scores 19, Winthrop beats Jacksonville St. 80-69
article.story: ROCK HILL, S.C. -- Xavier Cooks scored 19 points and grabbed eight rebounds and Joshua Davenport added 12 and 10 to help Winthrop beat Jacksonville State 80-69 Friday night. Cooks made 8 of 11 field goals and Davenport hit 5 of 7 while the rest of the team shot just 38 percent (17 of 45). Jimmy Gavin scored 13 and Keon Johnson had 12 points for Winthrop (3-0) which opened the season with three straight wins for the first time since the 2009-10 season. The Eagles took a 41-31 lead into halftime and a jumper by Roderick Perkins gave them a 17-point cushion with 11 minutes to play. Jacksonville State (2-3) answered with a 9-0 run to make it 61-53 fewer than two minutes later and trimmed its deficit to 6, 72-66, on a jumper by Malcolm Drumwright with 2:44 left, but Winthrop scored 8-straight to seal it. Erik Durham led Jacksonville State with 15 points.
